title: Keir Starmer delivers ‘sleepwalking’ national address on escalating Iran conflict
description: Spike Online Editor Tom Slater blasts UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer for his “non-announcement announcement” on the escalating war in Iran.   “This is one in a long line of non-announcement announcements from the Prime Minister in response to this war, but also generally,” Mr Slater told Sky News host Steve Price.  “He restates his position that the UK didn’t want to get dragged into the war, he announced a series of economic measures.   “He just underlined that in these times of conflict, energy crises, war, having Keir Starmer as Prime Minister of the UK is like bringing a spoon to a knife fight.  “He’s not made for these times, and he’s just made that abundantly clear with another kind of sleepwalking address that he’s just given.”

title: ‘What was the purpose of this?’: Albanese’s national address told Aussies nothing new
description: Sky News host Paul Murray discusses Prime Minister Anthony Albanese’s recent national address on the fuel crisis.   “So sorry, we had a national address from a prime minister to tell us nothing that we didn’t already know,” Mr Murray said.   “Three minutes, no announcement, nothing new.”

title: King Charles’ US trip seen as attempt to mend broken Trump-Starmer ties
description: Spike Online Editor Tom Slater says King Charles’ trip to the US is a clear attempt to repair the “broken” relationship between US President Donald Trump and UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer.  “It’s very clear that this tour has been sort of hastened as an attempt to get back on side with the Americans,” Mr Slater told Sky News host Steve Price.  “The relationship between Keir Starmer and Donald Trump has completely broken down.   “It’s another sign of how bad the transatlantic relationship has become that the King is being wheeled out once again.  “This is reflective of the fact that the government is increasingly adrift from what America is asking from us and what we can provide in the midst of this conflict.”

title: ‘That’s too bad’: Donald Trump responds to shocking Byron Noem’s scandalous pictures
description: Sky News host Rita Panahi reveals more details surrounding former Homeland Security chief Kristi Noem’s husband’s cross-dressing.   “The president has now responded … to this story,” Ms Panahi said.   “He’s told the Daily Mail he did not know about the photos and was surprised that Noem had confirmed their authenticity.”

title: High expectations set by past PM addresses left Albanese’s speech falling short
description: Broadcaster Lucy Zelic roasts Prime Minister Anthony Albanese’s “disappointing” national address  “It was just so disappointing,” Ms Zelic told Sky News host Paul Murray.   “I guess there had been so much buildup and anticipation, because historically we have come to expect when a prime minister declares that he is going to give a national address that there is going to be either new information or starkly important information that is really going to matter to the Australian people.  “He told us nothing we haven’t seen him screeching on the floors of parliament.”

title: ‘They s*** the bed’: Megyn Kelly says Democrats spooked by women candidates after Hillary and Kamala
description: 'The Megyn Kelly Show' host Megyn Kelly claims the Democrats are unlikely to nominate a woman for the presidential candidate because Hillary Clinton and Kamala Harris both “s*** the bed”.   “If you were given this assignment, like just open your playbook and pick one white Christian dude, you’d have him [Gavin Newsom] and Andy Beshear, that’s it,” Ms Kelly told Sky News host Paul Murray.   “You can’t go for a woman because they’re all spooked that they’ve had two female nominees, Hillary and then Kamala, both of whom have s*** the bed.  “It seems like it’s going to be Gavin Newsom, and that might be a smart choice because while he’s extremely far left, he’s got the hair, he’s got the widow’s peak, he’s tall, in the Democrat field, that’s a win.”

title: Phones and smartwatches to be banned in Victorian schools amid government crackdown
description: Sky News host James Macpherson reacts to the Victorian government's decision to ban smartwatches and other internet-connected devices in public and private schools.

title: Crowds scream as animatronic Olaf hilariously collapses in front of children
description: Sky News host Danica De Giorgio reacts to footage of an Olaf animatronic robot collapsing in front of crowds.  “Disneyland Paris they have a little animatronic version of the character Olaf from the Frozen film,” Ms De Giorgio said.   “The little robot malfunctioned.”

title: Megyn Kelly hilariously mocks Kristi Noem’s husband for leaked crossdressing photos
description: 'The Megyn Kelly Show' host Megyn Kelly mocks former Homeland Security chief Kristi Noem’s husband over leaked photos showing him crossdressing.  “Why? They’re so big, it’s a fetish … I don’t get how you’re into the enormous breasts, and you wear them,” Ms Kelly told Sky News host Paul Murray.   “What freaks me out about those Kristi Noem husband photos more than anything is the weird teeny tiny legs and teeny tiny waist and teeny tiny crotch area in the hot pink biker shorts and the bright blue super tight leggings showing off his rear end.   “I don’t know why she [Kristi Noem] doesn’t just get a divorce … why would you be married to a man with the fake breasts?  “She’s denying that she knew … don’t you think you’d know if your spouse had a habit of shoving enormous balloons in her dress or her pants and then doing dirty talk on the internet?  “It’s just bad on so many levels.”

title: ‘Is he buttering us up?’: Albanese’s national address hints at future restrictions
description: Sky News host Danica De Giorgio claims Prime Minister Anthony Albaense’s national address was a “grandstanding, ego boost for him” amid the fuel crisis.  “I’m not sure why the Prime Minister tried to gaslight us in a national address,” Ms De Giorgio said.  “My concern now is, is he buttering us up for restrictions coming in the future?”

title: ‘Complete nonsense’: Prosecutors' bullet claim in Charlie Kirk killing rejected
description: Filmmaker Ami Horowitz dismisses prosecutors’ claim that the bullet used to kill Charlie Kirk does not match the rifle linked to alleged murderer Tyler Robinson, calling it “complete nonsense”.   “All the evidence, and I mean, all the evidence, points to Robinson, like the DNA and everything, the fact that he actually admitted that he did this,” Mr Horowitz told Sky News host Rita Panahi.   “They said that the bullet doesn’t match the gun, yeah, that’s because the bullet disintegrated, it fragmented.  “This is all pointing toward this conspiracy theory that we’ve seen on the hard left and the hard right … it’s total and complete nonsense.”

title: Lefty Iran protest ‘all about the destruction of the West’
description: Filmmaker Ami Horowitz calls out a smug lefty for protesting for the military action against the Iranian regime to end.   “Do you think she cares one wit that the entire, and I mean, the entire Iranian diaspora is for this war and against the regime? Of course she doesn’t,” Mr Horowitz told Sky News host Rita Panahi.  “This is all about ultimately not about Iran, not about Palestine … this is all about the destruction and the burning down of the West, the destruction of capitalism.  “That’s how bad it’s got, and that’s really what the game is about.”

title: Albanese serves up ‘complete nothingburger’ in national address on fuel crisis
description: Sky News host James Macpherson claims Prime Minister Anthony Albanese served up a “complete nothingburger” in his national address on the fuel crisis.  “It was a complete nothingburger,” Mr Macpherson said.  “That he commandeered every television station in the country and radio broadcast for that, beggars’ belief.”

title: ‘Blasting Iran into oblivion’: Trump to give major nationwide address on Iran war
description: US President Donald Trump is scheduled to address America and the world at midday on April 2 on the ongoing war in Iran.  The US President previously announced Iran had asked for a ceasefire in the war.  He wrote on Truth Social: “Iran’s new regime president, much less radicalised and far more intelligent than his predecessors, has just asked the United States of America for a ceasefire.  “We will consider when the Hormuz Strait is open, free, and clear. Until then, we are blasting Iran into oblivion or, as they say, back to the Stone Ages.”

title: Australia to join talks on reopening the Strait of Hormuz
description: Australia is expected to join a 35-nation coalition to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz.  Britain will convene the international alliance, which will discuss diplomatic, political, and military options.  In a speech overnight, UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer said he would not cave into pressure from the US to join the war in Iran.
